cap_02_mechanism_fit_engine.name: Mechanism Fit Scoring — PATH A Engine
cap_02_mechanism_fit_engine.formula_status: PATH A — LOCKED 2026-04-28 by Fahad Kiani
cap_02_mechanism_fit_engine.path_b_status: PROHIBITED — cosine similarity with ‖p‖₂ in denominator is prohibited in all outputs

step_1_patient_vector_construction.description: Build an 8-dimensional patient vector p from the patient's genomic and clinical profile

axes.ddr: DNA damage response pathway activity — scored from ATM/BRCA1/BRCA2/PTEN mutation status, RS score if available
axes.mapk: RAS/RAF/MEK/ERK pathway activity — scored from KRAS/NRAS/BRAF mutation status and co-mutation context
axes.pi3k: PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathway activity — scored from PIK3CA/PTEN/AKT mutation status
axes.vegf: Angiogenesis/VEGF pathway activity — scored from tumor vascularity, bevacizumab history, HIF1A expression
axes.her2: HER2/ERBB2 pathway activity — scored from HER2 amplification/overexpression status
axes.io: Immune oncology permissiveness — scored from pTMB, Immunoscore-IC, CD8+ TIL density, MSS/MSI-H status, liver metastasis status
axes.efflux: Drug efflux/resistance pathway activity — scored from MDR1/ABCB1 expression, prior chemotherapy resistance
axes.rss: Replication stress score — scored from RS-High/RS-Low classification, CCNE1 amplification, CDK2 activity

step_2_trial_vector_construction.description: Build an 8-dimensional trial vector t from the trial's mechanism of action
step_2_trial_vector_construction.logic: Each axis value represents how strongly the trial's drug mechanism targets that biological pathway. A checkpoint inhibitor scores high on io axis. A MEK inhibitor scores high on mapk axis. A PARP inhibitor scores high on ddr axis.

example_break_crc001.rationale: STC-1010 is primarily an IO mechanism (whole-cell vaccine activating T-cell response) with secondary mapk relevance (KRAS-mutant CRC context)

step_3_dot_product.description: Compute the dot product p·t — the sum of axis-wise products
step_3_dot_product.formula: p·t = Σ(p_i × t_i) for i in {ddr, mapk, pi3k, vegf, her2, io, efflux, rss}
step_3_dot_product.what_it_measures: The raw alignment between patient biology and trial mechanism. High dot product = patient's active pathways match the trial's targeted pathways.

step_4_normalization.description: Divide by ‖t‖₂ — the L2 norm of the trial vector
step_4_normalization.why_normalize_by_trial_not_patient: Normalizing by ‖t‖₂ (not ‖p‖₂) means the score measures how well the patient matches the trial's requirements — not how active the patient's pathways are in absolute terms. A patient with very active pathways but misaligned with the trial still scores low. This is the key distinction from PATH B (cosine similarity), which normalizes by both vectors and loses this directionality.

step_5_clip.description: Clip to [0, 1] — scores cannot be negative or exceed 1
step_5_clip.ceiling: The theoretical ceiling is 1.0 when p and t are perfectly aligned. The practical ceiling for PATH A is 0.8898 (‖p_ref‖₂ constraint from reference patient vector).

step_6_output_labels.0.0_to_0.4: LOW — high failure risk; patient biology substantially misaligned with trial mechanism
step_6_output_labels.0.4_to_0.6: MODERATE — proceed with caution; partial alignment
step_6_output_labels.0.6_to_0.8: MODERATE-HIGH — favorable alignment; design gaps may still exist
step_6_output_labels.0.8_to_1.0: HIGH — strong fit; patient biology well-matched to trial mechanism

validated_runs.note: Only receipted two-layer demonstration. Responder score 0.8936 vs non-responder 0.6295. Delta value QUARANTINED (CT-03 — version conflict unresolved). Do not cite delta.

cap_04_target_lock.name: Target-Lock Scoring Engine (Layer 1)

eight_metastasis_steps.step_1: Local invasion — tumor cells breach basement membrane and invade surrounding stroma
eight_metastasis_steps.step_2: Intravasation — tumor cells enter blood or lymphatic vessels
eight_metastasis_steps.step_3: Survival in circulation — tumor cells resist anoikis and immune surveillance in bloodstream
eight_metastasis_steps.step_4: Arrest at distant site — tumor cells adhere to endothelium at metastatic site
eight_metastasis_steps.step_5: Extravasation — tumor cells exit vessels at distant site
eight_metastasis_steps.step_6: Micrometastasis formation — tumor cells survive and proliferate in foreign microenvironment
eight_metastasis_steps.step_7: Colonization — micrometastases establish vascular supply and immune evasion

step_1_evo2_7b.what_it_scores: Evolutionary constraint — how conserved is this gene across species? Highly conserved genes are more likely to be essential drivers. Evo2-7B computes the likelihood of the wild-type sequence vs. mutant sequence under evolutionary selection pressure.
step_1_evo2_7b.output: Evolutionary likelihood score [0, 1] — higher = more evolutionarily constrained = more likely to be a genuine driver

step_2_enformer.what_it_scores: Chromatin accessibility and gene regulatory activity — is this gene actively transcribed in the relevant cancer cell type? Enformer predicts ATAC-seq and ChIP-seq profiles from DNA sequence.
step_2_enformer.output: Regulatory activity score [0, 1] — higher = more actively regulated in cancer context

step_3_alphafold3.what_it_scores: Structural confidence — does this gene encode a protein with a well-defined 3D structure that can be targeted by a drug? Disordered proteins are harder to drug.
step_3_alphafold3.output: pLDDT structural confidence score [0, 100] — higher = more structured = more druggable

step_4_composite.description: Composite Target-Lock score from three model outputs
step_4_composite.rationale: Threshold set to maximize sensitivity for known FDA-approved targets while excluding false positives from the 38-gene panel

validated_performance.retroactive_validation: 9 FDA-approved genes validated retroactively in predictions_2024_retroactive.json

cap_04_target_lock.ceacam5_status: NOT IN 38-GENE PANEL — Target-Lock run has not been executed for CEACAM5. Any claim that CEACAM5 scores HIGH is EXPECTED (analyst inference), not COMPUTED. Must be labeled as prospective.

three_conditions_io_benefit_mss_crc.summary: CrisPRO's definitive finding: IO benefit in MSS CRC requires ALL THREE conditions simultaneously. Missing any one condition predicts failure.

condition_1_non_liver_metastatic.what: Patient must NOT have liver metastases at enrollment

why_biologically.mechanism: The liver is an immunosuppressive organ. Kupffer cells (liver-resident macrophages) constitutively express PD-L1 and secrete IL-10 and TGF-β, creating a tolerogenic microenvironment. Liver metastases recruit regulatory T cells (Tregs) and myeloid-derived suppressor cells (MDSCs) that suppress CD8+ T cell activity systemically — not just locally. A patient with liver metastases has systemic immune suppression that checkpoint inhibitors cannot overcome.
clinical_evidence: CO.26: liver-met PFS HR=1.39 (IO HARMFUL) vs no-LM PFS HR=0.54 (IO BENEFICIAL); Pint=0.02
REGONIVO US: 0/5 liver-met responders; non-liver-met ORR 22% (5/23)
REGONIVO Japan: all 8 CRC responders had NO liver metastases
RIN: NLM cohort ORR ~40% vs liver-met ORR ~5%
CAMILLA: post-hoc — patients without liver metastases had better outcomes
why_biologically.implication_for_trial_design: Liver metastasis status must be a pre-specified stratification variable. Trials that do not stratify by liver metastasis status will dilute the IO signal with immunosuppressed patients.

condition_1_non_liver_metastatic.prevalence_in_mss_mcrc: ~50–60% of mCRC patients have liver metastases at diagnosis. Restricting to NLM patients reduces the eligible population by ~50%.
condition_1_non_liver_metastatic.crispro_module: MOD-01 (Liver Metastasis Stratification) — EXECUTABLE_NOW — CRITICAL priority

condition_2_ptmb_28.what: Patient must have plasma TMB ≥28 mutations/Mb (GuardantOMNI assay)

why_biologically.mechanism: Tumor mutational burden (TMB) is a proxy for neoantigen load — the number of tumor-specific peptides that can be presented on MHC-I and recognized by T cells. High TMB tumors generate more neoantigens, creating more targets for checkpoint-released T cells. In MSS CRC, most tumors have low TMB — but the pTMB ≥28 subgroup (~15–20% of MSS mCRC) has sufficient neoantigen load for IO benefit.
why_biologically.why_plasma_not_tissue: Tissue TMB and plasma TMB are poorly correlated in CRC (Spearman r=0.13, p=0.20 — CO.26). Tissue TMB is confounded by tumor heterogeneity and archival tissue quality. Plasma TMB (pTMB) from ctDNA captures the mutational landscape of all metastatic sites simultaneously.
clinical_evidence: CO.26: pTMB ≥28 OS HR=0.34 (90% CI 0.18–0.63, p=0.022) — strongest IO signal from randomized data in MSS CRC
CO.26: pTMB vs tissue TMB Spearman r=0.13 (p=0.20) — tissue TMB is NOT a substitute
GRANITE GO-010: ctDNA-low subgroup HR 0.50 (directional benefit)
caveats: pTMB ≥28 threshold is data-driven (not pre-specified in CO.26 protocol)
GuardantOMNI-specific — not validated on other platforms
Derived from 2L+ refractory population — not validated in 1L

condition_2_ptmb_28.prevalence_in_mss_mcrc: ~15–20% of MSS mCRC patients have pTMB ≥28 (estimated from CO.26 subgroup)

condition_3_immune_inflamed_tme.what: Patient must have an immune-inflamed tumor microenvironment (high Immunoscore-IC, high CD8+ TIL density, low TGF-β suppression)

why_biologically.mechanism: Checkpoint inhibitors release the brakes on T cells — but T cells must already be present in the tumor to be released. MSS CRC tumors are predominantly 'immune excluded' or 'immune desert' — T cells are either blocked from entering the tumor or absent entirely. Only 'immune inflamed' tumors (T cells present at the tumor center and invasive margin) can respond to checkpoint blockade.

three_tme_phenotypes.immune_inflamed: CD8+ T cells present at tumor center AND invasive margin. High Immunoscore-IC. Responds to IO.
three_tme_phenotypes.immune_excluded: CD8+ T cells present at invasive margin but excluded from tumor center by stromal barriers (TGF-β, CAFs, decorin). Does not respond to IO alone — needs stromal barrier disruption.
three_tme_phenotypes.immune_desert: CD8+ T cells absent from both tumor center and invasive margin. Does not respond to IO.

clinical_evidence: AtezoTRIBE: Immunoscore-IC high HR ~0.50 (exploratory) vs Immunoscore-IC low HR ~0.90
MEDITREME: CD8-high + TGF-β-low subgroup showed best outcomes
GVAX: T-cell responses confirmed in peripheral blood but ORR 0% — T cells activated but cannot infiltrate tumor (immune excluded phenotype)

condition_3_immune_inflamed_tme.crispro_module: MOD-05 (Immunoscore-IC / CD8+ Baseline) — EXECUTABLE_NOW — HIGH priority

pharmacodynamic_dissociation.definition: T-cell activation in peripheral blood WITHOUT tumor regression. The immune system is activated but cannot translate activation into tumor killing.
pharmacodynamic_dissociation.mechanism: In MSS CRC, the tumor microenvironment creates multiple barriers between activated T cells and tumor cells: (1) stromal barriers (TGF-β, CAFs, decorin) physically exclude T cells from the tumor center; (2) immunosuppressive cells (Tregs, MDSCs) suppress T cell activity at the tumor site; (3) tumor cells downregulate MHC-I to avoid T cell recognition. Vaccines and checkpoint inhibitors can activate T cells in the periphery but cannot overcome these barriers.
canonical_examples: GVAX: ORR 0% despite confirmed T-cell responses to vaccine antigens in peripheral blood
PolyPEPI1018: T-cell responses to ≥1 antigen in 100% of patients; no objective responses
PANVAC + DC vaccine: T-cell responses >70% of patients; no OS benefit
AMPLIFY-201: mKRAS-specific T-cell responses confirmed; ctDNA clearance in 3/5 — but no objective responses
pharmacodynamic_dissociation.crispro_implication: Pharmacodynamic dissociation is the dominant failure mode for vaccine programs in MSS CRC. The fix is not a better vaccine — it is a stromal barrier disruption strategy (TGF-β inhibition, anti-FAP, bevacizumab normalization) combined with the vaccine.

immune_exclusion.definition: T cells are present at the tumor invasive margin but cannot penetrate the tumor center due to stromal barriers.
immune_exclusion.mechanism: Cancer-associated fibroblasts (CAFs) secrete TGF-β, which: (1) induces epithelial-mesenchymal transition (EMT) in tumor cells, increasing stromal density; (2) suppresses CD8+ T cell activity; (3) promotes regulatory T cell differentiation. Decorin and other extracellular matrix proteins create a physical barrier. The result: T cells accumulate at the tumor edge but cannot kill tumor cells at the center.
immune_exclusion.clinical_evidence: AtezoTRIBE: Immunoscore-IC high subgroup shows benefit (HR ~0.50) — these are the immune-inflamed patients. Immunoscore-IC low patients (immune excluded + immune desert) do not benefit.
immune_exclusion.crispro_implication: Immune exclusion is addressable — TGF-β inhibition (bintrafusp alfa, SHR-1701), anti-FAP agents, and bevacizumab (vascular normalization) can convert excluded tumors to inflamed. This is the rationale for combination strategies.

liver_metastasis_suppression.definition: Systemic immune suppression driven by liver metastases that prevents IO benefit even in patients with inflamed primary tumors.
liver_metastasis_suppression.mechanism: Liver metastases create a systemic immunosuppressive state through: (1) Kupffer cell PD-L1 expression and IL-10/TGF-β secretion; (2) recruitment of MDSCs and Tregs to the liver; (3) systemic elevation of immunosuppressive cytokines that suppress CD8+ T cell activity at all tumor sites. This is not a local effect — it suppresses immune responses throughout the body.
clinical_evidence: CO.26: liver-met PFS HR=1.39 (IO HARMFUL) vs no-LM PFS HR=0.54; Pint=0.02
REGONIVO US: 0/5 liver-met responders; non-liver-met ORR 22%
REGONIVO Japan: all 8 CRC responders had NO liver metastases
liver_metastasis_suppression.crispro_implication: Liver metastasis is the single most important stratification variable for IO trials in MSS CRC. It is not just a prognostic factor — it is a predictive biomarker of IO resistance.

mek_inhibition_failure.definition: MEK inhibition was hypothesized to increase tumor immunogenicity in MSS CRC by upregulating MHC-I and reducing immunosuppressive cytokines. This hypothesis was definitively refuted.
mek_inhibition_failure.mechanism_hypothesis: MEK inhibition was expected to: (1) upregulate MHC-I expression on tumor cells; (2) reduce VEGF and immunosuppressive cytokine production; (3) sensitize tumors to checkpoint blockade. The hypothesis was based on preclinical data.
mek_inhibition_failure.why_it_failed: IMblaze370 (Phase III, N=363): OS no significant difference across all three arms (atezo+cobi vs atezo vs rego). MEK inhibition did not increase tumor immunogenicity in clinical MSS CRC. The preclinical-to-clinical translation failed (D7 — Translational). Additionally, cobimetinib (MEKi) suppressed T cell proliferation — the combination was potentially antagonistic.
mek_inhibition_failure.clinical_evidence: IMblaze370: OS atezo+cobi 8.87 months vs atezo 7.10 months vs rego 8.51 months — no significant difference. D1 Biology (0.9) — MEK inhibition definitively fails in MSS CRC.
mek_inhibition_failure.crispro_implication: MEK inhibition is not a viable IO sensitization strategy in MSS CRC. Any abstract proposing MEK+IO in MSS CRC should be flagged as repeating a definitively failed hypothesis.

stc1010_mechanism.platform_name: STC-1010 — Stimulated Tumor Cells (STC) allogeneic whole-cell therapeutic vaccine
stc1010_mechanism.what_it_is: STC-1010 is an allogeneic (off-the-shelf) whole-cell therapeutic vaccine composed of three irradiated CRC cell lines (CT26 murine CRC, CMT-93 rectal carcinoma, LTPA pancreatic adenocarcinoma) that have been haptenated with DNFB (2,4-dinitrofluorobenzene) and modified to secrete GM-CSF. The vaccine is designed to activate the immune system against colorectal cancer antigens by exploiting the hapten-specific immune response.

step_1_haptenation.what: DNFB (2,4-dinitrofluorobenzene) is covalently conjugated to the surface proteins of the tumor cell lines
step_1_haptenation.why: DNFB is a contact sensitizer — it creates a strong hapten-specific immune response. When DNFB-conjugated tumor cells are injected, the immune system recognizes the DNFB hapten as foreign and mounts a vigorous immune response. This response is then redirected against the tumor antigens on the cell surface.
step_1_haptenation.mechanism: DNFB reacts with lysine residues on cell surface proteins, creating DNFB-protein conjugates. These conjugates are recognized by DNFB-specific T cells (pre-sensitized by prior DNFB skin sensitization). The DNFB-specific T cells attack the haptenated tumor cells, releasing tumor antigens that are then processed by dendritic cells.
step_1_haptenation.sc_001_conflict: SC-001 ACTIVE CONFLICT: CT26 carries BRAF V600E (ATCC/CCLE/COSMIC). BreAK CRC-001 requires BRAF wild-type patients (Exclusion Criterion #7). This scientific inconsistency must be flagged.

step_2_gm_csf_secretion.what: The tumor cell lines are modified to secrete GM-CSF (granulocyte-macrophage colony-stimulating factor)
step_2_gm_csf_secretion.why: GM-CSF recruits and activates dendritic cells (DCs) at the injection site. DCs are the professional antigen-presenting cells that process tumor antigens and present them to T cells in the lymph nodes.
step_2_gm_csf_secretion.mechanism: GM-CSF creates a local inflammatory environment that: (1) recruits immature DCs to the injection site; (2) promotes DC maturation and upregulation of MHC-II and co-stimulatory molecules (CD80, CD86); (3) enhances antigen uptake and processing; (4) promotes DC migration to draining lymph nodes.

step_3_cyclophosphamide_preconditioning.what: Low-dose cyclophosphamide is administered before vaccination
step_3_cyclophosphamide_preconditioning.why: Cyclophosphamide at low doses selectively depletes regulatory T cells (Tregs) without suppressing effector T cells. Tregs suppress anti-tumor immune responses — their depletion allows the vaccine-induced T cells to expand and function.
step_3_cyclophosphamide_preconditioning.mechanism: Tregs express high levels of CD25 (IL-2 receptor alpha) and are more sensitive to cyclophosphamide than effector T cells. Low-dose cyclophosphamide (300 mg/m²) depletes Tregs by ~50% while preserving effector T cell function.

step_4_icd_cascade.what: Immunogenic Cell Death (ICD) — the irradiated, haptenated tumor cells die in a way that activates the immune system
step_4_icd_cascade.why: Not all cell death is immunogenic. ICD releases damage-associated molecular patterns (DAMPs) that activate the innate immune system and promote adaptive immune responses.

damps_released.calreticulin: Translocates from ER to cell surface — acts as 'eat me' signal for DCs
damps_released.HMGB1: Released from nucleus — activates TLR4 on DCs, promoting DC maturation
damps_released.ATP: Released from dying cells — activates P2X7 receptors on DCs, promoting NLRP3 inflammasome activation and IL-1β secretion
damps_released.HSP70: Released from dying cells — activates TLR2/4 on DCs, promoting DC maturation and antigen cross-presentation

step_5_t_cell_priming.what: Mature DCs migrate to draining lymph nodes and present tumor antigens to naive T cells
step_5_t_cell_priming.why: T cell priming requires three signals: (1) antigen presentation (MHC-peptide + TCR); (2) co-stimulation (CD80/86 + CD28); (3) cytokine signal (IL-12 for Th1/CTL differentiation). The vaccine provides all three.
step_5_t_cell_priming.expected_outcome: CD8+ cytotoxic T lymphocytes (CTLs) specific for tumor antigens are generated and expand. These CTLs circulate and kill CEACAM5+, CDK1+, EPHA2+ tumor cells.

step_6_tumor_killing.what: Vaccine-primed CTLs infiltrate the tumor and kill tumor cells
step_6_tumor_killing.challenge_in_mss_crc: This is where pharmacodynamic dissociation occurs. Even if CTLs are generated (step 5), they must: (1) traffic to the tumor; (2) penetrate the stromal barrier; (3) overcome immunosuppression in the TME; (4) kill tumor cells faster than the tumor grows. In MSS CRC, steps 2 and 3 are the primary barriers.

key_antigens.CEACAM5: Carcinoembryonic antigen — expressed in 98.7% of CRC. Primary target.
key_antigens.CDK1: Cyclin-dependent kinase 1 — overexpressed in proliferating tumor cells
key_antigens.EPHA2: Ephrin type-A receptor 2 — promotes tumor invasion and metastasis

stc1010_mechanism.why_mss_crc_is_hard: MSS CRC is immunologically 'cold' — low neoantigen load, immune-excluded or immune-desert TME, high stromal density, liver metastasis-driven systemic immunosuppression. STC-1010 addresses the T-cell priming step but does not directly address the stromal barrier or liver metastasis suppression. This is why the three conditions for IO benefit (NLM, pTMB ≥28, inflamed TME) are critical for BreAK CRC-001.

path_a_fit_derivation_break_crc001.label: MODERATE-HIGH

patient_vector_p.source: p_vector_locked.json — MSS mCRC patient profile (Alzeeb et al. 2024 cohort)

axis_rationale.ddr: MSS CRC has low DDR deficiency — BRCA mutations rare, RS score low in most patients
axis_rationale.mapk: KRAS mutations in ~40% of MSS mCRC — high MAPK pathway activity
axis_rationale.pi3k: PIK3CA mutations in ~15–20% of CRC — moderate PI3K activity
axis_rationale.vegf: Bevacizumab backbone — VEGF pathway relevant; liver metastases increase VEGF
axis_rationale.her2: HER2 amplification rare in MSS CRC (~3–5%)
axis_rationale.io: MSS CRC is IO-cold — low pTMB, immune-excluded TME. io=0.40 reflects the minority of patients with inflamed TME
axis_rationale.efflux: Prior chemotherapy exposure — some efflux resistance expected
axis_rationale.rss: Low RS score in MSS CRC — CCNE1 amplification rare

trial_vector_t.source: STC-1010 mechanism of action — IO-primary, mapk-secondary

axis_rationale.io: STC-1010 is primarily an IO mechanism — whole-cell vaccine activating T-cell response. io=0.80 reflects the primary mechanism.
axis_rationale.mapk: KRAS-mutant CRC context — mapk=0.30 reflects secondary relevance (KRAS-mutant tumors may have different vaccine antigen profiles)
axis_rationale.vegf: mFOLFOX6 + bevacizumab backbone — vegf=0.20 reflects bevacizumab component
axis_rationale.ddr: No DDR component in STC-1010 mechanism

path_a_fit_derivation_break_crc001.interpretation: MODERATE-HIGH alignment. The io axis is the primary driver — STC-1010's vaccine mechanism aligns with the io permissiveness of the MSS mCRC patient profile. The mapk axis is secondary — KRAS-mutant CRC context provides additional alignment. The score of 0.7375 indicates favorable but not optimal alignment — the 4 design gaps explain why the score is not higher.

gap_1.name: Liver Metastasis Not Pre-Specified as Stratification Variable
gap_1.what: BreAK CRC-001 does not pre-specify liver metastasis status as a stratification variable in the randomization scheme
gap_1.why_critical: CO.26 showed liver-met PFS HR=1.39 (IO HARMFUL) vs no-LM HR=0.54 (Pint=0.02). REGONIVO US: 0/5 liver-met responders. If BreAK CRC-001 enrolls liver-met patients without stratification, the IO signal from NLM patients will be diluted by immunosuppressed liver-met patients. This is the same failure mode as CO.26.
gap_1.consequence_if_not_addressed: Trial may fail to detect IO benefit even if STC-1010 works in NLM patients. The responder subgroup (NLM + pTMB ≥28) will be diluted.
gap_1.fix: Add liver metastasis status as a pre-specified stratification variable in the randomization scheme. Consider NLM-enriched enrollment (≥50% NLM patients) or NLM-only cohort.

gap_2.name: pTMB Not Collected Prospectively
gap_2.what: BreAK CRC-001 does not include prospective pTMB measurement (GuardantOMNI or equivalent)
gap_2.why_high: CO.26 pTMB ≥28 OS HR=0.34 (90% CI 0.18–0.63, p=0.022) — the strongest IO enrichment signal in MSS CRC from randomized data. Without prospective pTMB collection, BreAK CRC-001 cannot identify the pTMB ≥28 subgroup that is most likely to benefit from STC-1010.
gap_2.consequence_if_not_addressed: Cannot perform the pTMB ≥28 subgroup analysis that would identify the responder subpopulation. Retrospective pTMB from archival plasma is unreliable.
gap_2.fix: Add GuardantOMNI (or equivalent ctDNA panel) at baseline as a mandatory biomarker collection. Pre-specify pTMB ≥28 as an exploratory endpoint.

gap_3.name: IO Axis Alignment Moderate (patient io=0.40 below optimal ≥0.50)
gap_3.what: The patient vector io axis value (0.40) is below the optimal threshold (≥0.50) for IO-based mechanisms
gap_3.why_medium: The io axis value of 0.40 reflects the MSS CRC population average — most patients have immune-excluded or immune-desert TME. The optimal patient for STC-1010 has io ≥0.50 (immune-inflamed TME). Enrolling patients with io=0.40 means ~60% of patients have suboptimal TME for vaccine response.
gap_3.consequence_if_not_addressed: Moderate dilution of the IO signal. Not as critical as GAP-01 and GAP-02 because the io axis is already the primary driver.
gap_3.fix: Add Immunoscore-IC or CD8+ TIL density as a baseline biomarker. Consider enriching for Immunoscore-IC high patients.

gap_4.name: No Biomarker Gate for Patient Selection
gap_4.what: BreAK CRC-001 has no pre-specified biomarker gate for patient selection — all MSS mCRC patients are eligible regardless of IO permissiveness
gap_4.why_high: Without a biomarker gate, the trial enrolls both IO-responsive (NLM + pTMB ≥28 + inflamed TME) and IO-resistant (liver-met + pTMB <28 + excluded TME) patients. The IO-resistant patients dilute the signal from IO-responsive patients.
gap_4.consequence_if_not_addressed: The trial may fail to detect IO benefit even if STC-1010 works in the IO-responsive subpopulation. This is the same failure mode as QUILT-2.004 (HR 1.061, p=0.91).
gap_4.fix: Implement a two-gate patient selection framework: Gate 1 = non-liver-metastatic; Gate 2 = pTMB ≥28 OR Immunoscore-IC high. This reduces the eligible population but increases the probability of detecting a signal.

biomarker_biology_6domains.description: CrisPRO's 6-domain suppression landscape for MSS CRC — the biological barriers that prevent IO benefit

domain_1_immune_exclusion.name: Immune Exclusion
domain_1_immune_exclusion.mechanism: TGF-β secreted by CAFs creates a stromal barrier that physically excludes CD8+ T cells from the tumor center. T cells accumulate at the invasive margin but cannot penetrate.
domain_1_immune_exclusion.clinical_evidence: AtezoTRIBE: Immunoscore-IC high HR ~0.50 vs low HR ~0.90. MEDITREME: CD8-high + TGF-β-low subgroup best outcomes.
domain_1_immune_exclusion.addressable_by: TGF-β inhibition (bintrafusp alfa, SHR-1701), anti-FAP agents, bevacizumab (vascular normalization)

domain_2_liver_metastasis_suppression.name: Liver Metastasis Systemic Immunosuppression
domain_2_liver_metastasis_suppression.mechanism: Kupffer cells (liver-resident macrophages) constitutively express PD-L1 and secrete IL-10 and TGF-β. Liver metastases recruit MDSCs and Tregs systemically.
domain_2_liver_metastasis_suppression.clinical_evidence: CO.26: liver-met PFS HR=1.39 (HARMFUL) vs no-LM HR=0.54; Pint=0.02. REGONIVO US: 0/5 liver-met responders.
domain_2_liver_metastasis_suppression.addressable_by: Liver metastasis resection (if feasible), liver-directed therapy (TACE, SBRT), NLM patient selection

domain_3_low_neoantigen_load.name: Low Neoantigen Load (MSS)
domain_3_low_neoantigen_load.mechanism: MSS CRC has intact mismatch repair — low mutation rate → low neoantigen load → few tumor-specific peptides for T cell recognition. pTMB ≥28 identifies the minority of MSS patients with sufficient neoantigen load.
key_markers: Tissue TMB (note: poor correlation with pTMB in CRC, r=0.13)
domain_3_low_neoantigen_load.clinical_evidence: CO.26: pTMB ≥28 OS HR=0.34 (90% CI 0.18–0.63). pTMB vs tissue TMB Spearman r=0.13 (p=0.20).
domain_3_low_neoantigen_load.addressable_by: pTMB ≥28 patient selection; DNMT inhibition (guadecitabine — failed in GVAX combination)

domain_4_treg_suppression.name: Regulatory T Cell Suppression
domain_4_treg_suppression.mechanism: Tregs suppress CD8+ T cell activity in the TME via IL-10, TGF-β, and CTLA-4-mediated co-stimulation blockade. High Treg density correlates with poor IO response.
domain_4_treg_suppression.clinical_evidence: Cyclophosphamide preconditioning in STC-1010 protocol — low-dose cyclophosphamide depletes Tregs by ~50%.
domain_4_treg_suppression.addressable_by: Low-dose cyclophosphamide (STC-1010 protocol), anti-CTLA-4 (ipilimumab, tremelimumab), anti-CCR4 (mogamulizumab)

domain_5_mhc_downregulation.name: MHC-I Downregulation
domain_5_mhc_downregulation.mechanism: Tumor cells downregulate MHC-I expression to avoid T cell recognition. This is a common immune escape mechanism in MSS CRC.
domain_5_mhc_downregulation.clinical_evidence: MHC-I loss is present in ~15–20% of CRC tumors. Patients with MHC-I loss cannot respond to T cell-based therapies.
domain_5_mhc_downregulation.addressable_by: DNMT inhibition (upregulates MHC-I), IFN-γ signaling restoration, B2M mutation exclusion

domain_6_mdsc_suppression.name: Myeloid-Derived Suppressor Cell (MDSC) Suppression
domain_6_mdsc_suppression.mechanism: MDSCs are immature myeloid cells that suppress T cell activity via arginase-1, iNOS, and reactive oxygen species. High MDSC density in MSS CRC TME.
domain_6_mdsc_suppression.clinical_evidence: High MDSC density correlates with poor IO response in MSS CRC. Liver metastases increase MDSC recruitment.
domain_6_mdsc_suppression.addressable_by: Anti-VEGF (bevacizumab reduces MDSC recruitment), CSF1R inhibitors, IDO inhibitors

escapemap_ov_methodology.name: EscapeMap — Bevacizumab Biomarker Stratification (Ovarian)
escapemap_ov_methodology.what_escapemap_is: EscapeMap is CrisPRO's analysis of ZEB1 as a prognostic biomarker in the bevacizumab arm of ovarian cancer trials. It uses Cox regression, median-split, and tertile analyses to characterize the ZEB1-bevacizumab relationship.

methodology.dataset: GOG-0218 (bevacizumab arm) — ovarian cancer Phase III trial

continuous_cox_model.description: Cox proportional hazards model with ZEB1 expression as a continuous variable
continuous_cox_model.interpretation: Each unit increase in ZEB1 expression is associated with a 48% increase in the hazard of death in the bevacizumab arm. ZEB1-high patients have worse OS on bevacizumab.

multivariable_cox.result: HR=1.5247 (95% CI [1.1256, 2.0652], p=0.006447) — adjusted for covariates
multivariable_cox.interpretation: ZEB1 is an independent prognostic factor after adjusting for other clinical variables.

arm_interaction.interpretation: PROGNOSTIC, NOT ARM-SPECIFIC. The arm interaction p=0.246 means ZEB1 is prognostic (predicts OS regardless of treatment) but NOT predictive of bevacizumab benefit specifically. ZEB1-high patients do worse on bevacizumab, but they also do worse on chemotherapy alone.
arm_interaction.mandatory_wording: ZEB1 is a prognostic marker in the bevacizumab arm, not a predictive marker of bevacizumab benefit. The arm interaction is not statistically significant (p=0.246).

median_split.result: HR=2.500 (95% CI [1.322, 4.727], log-rank p=0.0036, FDR=0.0499) — bevacizumab arm OS
median_split.interpretation: ZEB1-high patients (above median) have 2.5× higher hazard of death than ZEB1-low patients in the bevacizumab arm.

tertile_analysis.interpretation: Top tertile ZEB1 vs bottom tertile: 2.5× higher hazard of death.

hif1a_result.interpretation: HIF1A is also prognostic in the bevacizumab arm — PLATFORM_COMPRESSION signal (multiple EMT/angiogenesis markers are prognostic).

m2_stratum.result: M2 beneficial median OS: 34.4 months (CI 32.7–38.8), p=9.52×10⁻⁵
m2_stratum.mandatory_wording: Preparatory M2-bevacizumab biomarker panel for downstream descriptive and conditioning analyses — not a validated prognostic signature until dbGaP-linked patient-level outcomes are available. (LOCKED 2026-05-12)

gbm_permanently_closed.reason: Three datasets all null or contradicted:

datasets.result: delta=−0.34, p=0.000184, d=−0.31 — ZEB1 DECREASES in recurrent GBM (opposite of escape hypothesis)
delta=−0.431, p=0.0334 — ZEB1 DECREASES in recurrent GBM

gbm_permanently_closed.operational_meaning: Do not apply EscapeMap to GBM. Do not reopen without new evidence. GBM ZEB1 is not an escape marker — it decreases in recurrent GBM, which is the opposite of the escape hypothesis.

what_crispro_proved: OV ZEB1 continuous Cox: HR=1.4803 (95% CI [1.1188, 1.9586], p=0.006042) — bevacizumab arm OS
OV ZEB1 multivariable Cox: HR=1.5247 (95% CI [1.1256, 2.0652], p=0.006447)
OV ZEB1 arm interaction: HR=1.243, p=0.246 — PROGNOSTIC, NOT ARM-SPECIFIC
OV ZEB1 median split: HR=2.500 (95% CI [1.322, 4.727], FDR=0.0499)
OV HIF1A: HR=2.245 (95% CI [1.172, 4.300], p=0.0147) — PLATFORM_COMPRESSION
M2 stratum calibration: GEO 74.0% vs published 73.9% (within 0.1%)
